ne3.title: Regional NE3 Distribution
ne3.description: FTTH distribution network: fiber blowing, splicing in POP and DP, measurements, documentation and technical handover.

detail.intro: NE3 covers distribution before the final customer connection. The core work is blowing fiber, splicing in POP and DP, measuring where applicable and documenting the network for NE4 continuation or certification.
detail.step1: Segment preparation: review plans, involved POP/DP, ducts, chambers, cabinets, permits/access and real site conditions before intervention.
detail.step2: Blowing/pulling: prepare ducts, lubrication/guide where applicable, blow fiber/cable and control the route according to design.
detail.step3: Network splicing and order: splices in POP, DP, closures or cabinets, fiber identification and physical organization for operations.
detail.step4: Measurement and closeout: tests where in scope, photos, incidents, as-built/redlines, open points and technical handover.
detail.output1: Segment record with location, status, photos and technical notes.
detail.output2: Open items separated by cause: access, material, damage, civil work, documentation or client coordination.
detail.output3: Closeout package for QA, internal certification, NE4 continuity or billing preparation.
detail.signal1: Every segment has a clear state: ready, partial, blocked or pending third parties.
detail.signal2: Splices, measurements and photos are linked to the correct physical point.
detail.signal3: The handover prevents the next crew from reconstructing the story in WhatsApp.

ne4.title: NE4 final FTTH connection
ne4.description: Final FTTH connection from DP/HÜP to GF-TA/ONT: indoor route, splice or termination, QA photos and verifiable technical closeout.

detail.intro: NE4 is the final connection inside the customer environment. What matters is validating access and route, working from DP/HÜP to GF-TA/ONT where applicable, terminating correctly and leaving clear evidence for QA, second visit or billing.
detail.step1: Validate appointment, address, access, building type, DP/HÜP and feasible indoor route.
detail.step2: Prepare the indoor drop or relevant segment: entry point, conduit/drilling where applicable and route protection.
detail.step3: Mount or prepare GF-TA/ONT with splice/connectorization and technical check before closeout.
detail.step4: Close with photos, blocker notes, visit evidence and final status for QA, billing or second visit.
detail.output1: Address-level report with DP/HÜP, internal route, GF-TA/ONT, before/after photos and visit result.
detail.output2: Open points classified by cause: no access, customer absent, blocked route, missing material, civil work, damage or activation out of scope.
detail.output3: Technical closeout ready for coordination: completed, partial, blocked or second visit required.
detail.signal1: The DP → HÜP → GF-TA → ONT chain is visible instead of buried in loose notes.
detail.signal2: QA photos connect each physical component to the right address and technician.
detail.signal3: Statuses allow second visits to be planned without WhatsApp archaeology.

workManager.title: Work Manager
workManager.description: Internal PWA for fiber operations: appointments, team PINs, offline queue, field reports, PDF/Excel export and project status flow.

detail.intro: Work Manager is the internal PWA for NE4 field teams. It centralizes appointments, technician PIN access, reports, offline queue, evidence and exports so information does not disappear between WhatsApp, Excel and loose notes.
detail.step1: Coordination loads or syncs appointments and team assignments.
detail.step2: Technicians enter with PIN, see their work and complete the report on mobile.
detail.step3: The app stores evidence and data offline when field connectivity fails.
detail.step4: Coordination exports PDF/Excel and updates project status.
detail.output2: Offline queue to reduce field information loss.
detail.output3: Exports and states useful for coordination and administration.
detail.signal1: Less dependency on chats for critical information.
detail.signal2: Simple technician access without full-account friction.
detail.signal3: Data ready for QA, follow-up and reporting.

fincontrol.title: FinControl
fincontrol.description: Financial control app for our operation: transactions, cost centers, accounts receivable/payable, cash signals and internal reporting.

detail.intro: FinControl organizes the operational finance layer: transactions, projects, cost centers, accounts receivable, accounts payable and internal reports. It does not replace official accounting; it gives daily operational visibility for clearer decisions.
detail.step1: Record income, expenses, accounts and classification by project or cost center.
detail.step2: Track AR/AP and relevant dates for cash-flow visibility.
detail.step3: Dashboard with charts and signals for liquidity, costs and obligations.
detail.step4: Internal PDF reports for administrative review and decision-making.
detail.output1: Operational view of cash, payments, receivables and projects.
detail.output2: Internal reports for administrative and financial discussions.
detail.output3: Data structure ready to grow toward accounting automation.
detail.signal2: Receivables and payables are visible before they hurt.
detail.signal3: Reports are internal and traceable, not decorative.

nas.title: NAS: drop to customer home
nas.description: Customer connection from the existing drop through garden/exterior route to the home, with entry, equipment mounting, QA photos and technical closeout.

detail.intro: NAS is the work of bringing the connection from the existing drop to the customer home. The essence: garden/exterior route, house entry, HÜP/GF-TA/ONT mounting where applicable and clean closeout documentation.
detail.step1: Review appointment, available drop, garden/exterior route, home access and entry point.
detail.step2: Execute the exterior route: trench or light conduit where applicable, protect microduct/cable and enter through facade or basement.
detail.step3: Mount HÜP, GF-TA and ONT/router where applicable, with termination, splice/connectorization and tidy cabling.
detail.step4: Close with before/after photos, equipment evidence, real blocker if any and status for QA or billing.
detail.output1: Customer report with exterior route, entry point, mounted equipment and QA photos.
detail.output2: Real open points: blocked garden/route, customer absent, missing material, indoor access, damage or activation out of scope.
detail.output3: Technical closeout ready for coordination: installed, partial, blocked or second visit required.
detail.signal1: The drop → garden → home → equipment route is clear and verifiable.
detail.signal2: Photos show both exterior work and indoor equipment mounting.
detail.signal3: Final status supports QA, second-visit planning and billing without inventing anything.

densification.title: Final Customer Connections
densification.description: Final FTTH connections in already built areas: appointments, missing drops, customer-side installation, evidence and second visits.

detail.intro: Densification closes customers in already built areas. The focus is organizing appointments, solving access, executing final connections, documenting evidence and making clear what is complete or needs a second visit.
detail.step1: Review address, network availability, previous state and appointment window.
detail.step2: Coordinate customer/crew and prepare the final FTTH work.
detail.step3: Execute final connection, installation or review according to scope and site conditions.
detail.step4: Close with result, evidence, open items and status for follow-up or billing.
detail.output1: Customer/address record with final state or blocker reason.
detail.output2: Closeout evidence and notes for next actions.
detail.output3: Clean base to measure internal progress without publishing sensitive KPIs.
detail.signal1: Failed appointments are documented, not hidden.
detail.signal2: Second visits come from a real state, not memory.
detail.signal3: Each closeout has enough evidence for administrative control.

teamSection.label: Leadership

teamSection.description: The company combines administrative leadership in Germany with technical execution and systems development for fiber projects. The structure is small, direct and focused on measurable delivery.

Operational structure so projects can be executed clearly

dualMap.label: Geography
dualMap.title: Operations in Germany.
dualMap.subtitle: The company operates from Celle and is supported by software development from Bogotá. The model is simple: field work creates information; software organizes it for project control, quality and administration.

germany.description: Celle concentrates operational coordination: FTTH projects, field teams, NAS/access, final customer connections, splicing, quality control and documentation.

colombia.description: Bogotá supports development of the internal tools: Lumen, Work Manager, FinControl and automations for customer intake and follow-up.
